audit_status.source_base: Greek Galatians text tradition, draft aligned to public-domain comparison layer later
audit_status.review_note: Compact complete draft reviewed for urgent opening, no rival good news, human approval, apocalypse/calling of Jesus Messiah, former zeal, Arabia/Damascus/Jerusalem narrative.

verses.restored: Paul, apostle not from humans nor through a human, but through Jesus Messiah and Divine Father, who raised him from the dead,
and all the siblings with me, to the assemblies of Galatia:
Grace to you and peace from Divine our Father and Lord Jesus Messiah,
who gave himself for our sins/misalignments, so that he might rescue us from the present harmful age, according to the will of our Divine and Father,
to whom be glory into the ages of the ages. Amen.
I marvel that you are so quickly being transferred from the One who called you in the grace of Messiah to a different good news,
which is not another; except there are some troubling you and wanting to distort the good news of Messiah.
But even if we or a messenger from heaven should announce good news to you beyond what we announced to you, let that one be devoted to judgment.
As we have said before, and now again I say: if anyone announces good news to you beyond what you received, let that one be devoted to judgment.
For now am I persuading humans or the Divine? Or am I seeking to please humans? If I were still pleasing humans, I would not be servant of Messiah.
For I make known to you, siblings, the good news announced by me, that it is not according to human.
For I neither received it from a human nor was taught it, but through an unveiling/apocalypse of Jesus Messiah.
For you heard of my former conduct in Judaism, that beyond measure I pursued/persecuted the assembly of the Divine and ravaged it,
and I advanced in Judaism beyond many of my own age among my people, being more exceedingly zealous for the traditions of my fathers.
But when the Divine, the One who set me apart from my mother's womb and called me through his grace, was pleased
to unveil his Son in me, so that I might announce him as good news among the nations, immediately I did not consult flesh and blood,
nor did I go up to Jerusalem to those apostles before me, but I went away into Arabia and again returned to Damascus.
Then after three years I went up to Jerusalem to visit Cephas, and I remained with him fifteen days.
But I saw none of the other apostles except James, the brother of the Lord.
Now what I write to you, look, before the Divine, I do not lie.
Then I went into the regions of Syria and Cilicia.
But I was unknown by face to the assemblies of Judea that are in Messiah.
They were only hearing: The one formerly pursuing/persecuting us now announces as good news the trust he once ravaged.
And they glorified the Divine in me.

translation_notes: Apokalypsis in verse 12 is rendered unveiling/apocalypse to preserve revelatory force.
Paul's critique of his former zeal is autobiographical and rhetorical; it should not become contempt for Judaism or Jewish tradition.
